revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikota-jinja]][[ja:伊居太神社 (尼崎市)]] {{Expand Japanese|伊居太神社 (尼崎市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikota Shrine | native_name = 伊居太神社 | image = Ikotajinja01.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikota Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.747197|135.43382}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|de|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|eo|Takemikazuĉi|es|Takemikazuchi|fa|تاکه‌میکازوچی|fr|Takemikazuchi|id|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ|pl|Takemikazuchi|pt|Takemikadzuchi|ru|Такэмикадзути|zh|建御雷|qid=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikota Shrine'''|伊居太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q125316983|ltq=Q125316983|qid=Q125316983|1=Kasuga shrine (worship)}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Amagasaki|lt=Amagasaki|ar|أماغاساكي (هيوغو)|arz|اماجاساكى, هيوجو|azb|آماقاساکی، هیوقو|az|Amaqasaki|be|Амагасакі|bg|Амагасаки|ca|Amagasaki|ceb|Amagasaki (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Амагасаки|cs|Amagasaki|de|Amagasaki|el|Αμαγκασάκι|en|Amagasaki|envoyage|Amagasaki|eo|Amagasaki|es|Amagasaki (Hyōgo)|et|Amagasaki|eu|Amagasaki|fa|آماگاساکی، Hyōgo|ko|아마가사키시|lt|Amagasakis|mg|Amagasaki|mr|आमागासाकी|ms|Amagasaki|mzn|آماگاساکی، هیوگو|nl|Amagasaki|no|Amagasaki|pl|Amagasaki|pt|Amagasaki|ro|Amagasaki|ru|Амагасаки|sco|Amagasaki|sh|Amagasaki|simple|Amagasaki|sr|Амагасаки|sv|Amagasaki|szl|Amagasaki|tg|Амагасаки|th|อามางาซากิ|tl|Amagasaki|tr|Amagasaki|tt|Амагасаки|tum|Amagasaki|uk|Амагасакі|ur|اماگاساکی، ہیوگو|uz|Amagasaki|vec|Amagasaky|vi|Amagasaki|war|Amagasaki|wuu|尼崎市|zh_classical|尼崎市|zh_min_nan|Amagasaki-chhī|zh_yue|尼崎|zh|尼崎市|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}} * {{ill|Q864163|ltq=Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Kawabe District, Hyōgo|lt=Kawabe district|ceb|Kawanabe-gun|en|Kawabe District, Hyōgo|es|Distrito de Kawabe|fr|District de Kawabe|id|Distrik Kawabe, Hyōgo|it|Distretto di Kawabe|ja|川辺郡|ko|가와베군|pl|Powiat Kawabe|ru|Кавабе (уезд, Хёго)|zh|川邊郡|qid=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} * 181063 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いこたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005011278 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikota-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Iketano Shrine|lt=Iketano Shrine|qid=Q135039219|1=Iketano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県尼崎市下坂部4丁目13-26', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府池田市綾羽', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ikota-jinja}} [[JA:伊居太神社 (尼崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11379327}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ikota Shrine |Image = [[File:Ikota-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}}Shimosakabe 4-chōme 13-26 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|44|50.2|N|135|26|1.6|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}
{{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) disputed shrine |Founded = (Traditionally) 77th year of the reign of the 16th Emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|qid=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Ikota Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{Osm box|w|508932027}} '''Ikota Shrine''' (いこたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shimosakabe, {{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=disputed shrines|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} identified as the {{ill|lt=Ikota Shrine|Q11379328|qid=Q11379328|1=Ikota Shrine}} listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} (the other being {{ill|lt=Ikeda City|Q817965|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}’s {{ill|lt=Ikota Shrine (Ikeda City)|Q11379329|qid=Q11379329|lt=Ikota Shrine|1=Ikeda Shrine}} (Ikeda Shrine)). === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} * {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * Jointly enshrined: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === History === The area was the residence of the {{ill|lt=Sakaibe clan|Q85878332|qid=Q85878332|1=Sakaibe Clan}}, and the shrine originally enshrined the clan’s {{ill|lt=chieftain|Q11902879|qid=Q11902879|1=Lord mayor}}, {{ill|lt=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto|Q11435420|qid=Q11435420|1=Ōhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and his descendants. It is believed that when the {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|qid=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}} came to control the area, they invited ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}}) the deities of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|qid=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} here.Ikota Shrine, “Coastline and Origins” (1999). “Osaka-fu Zenshi” identifies the {{ill|lt=Ikota Shrine (Ikeda City)|Q11379329|qid=Q11379329|lt=Ikota Shrine|1=Ikeda Shrine}} in Ikeda City as the Shikinaisha, stating that it was relocated ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=senzā|1=UNKNOWN}}) from Kosakata Village in {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Itami City|Q335444|qid=Q335444|1=Itami}}) to {{ill|lt=Toyono District|Q1205516|qid=Q1205516|1=Toyono District, Osaka}}. This view follows the theory of “Settsushi,” but there is also the argument that “judging from the {{ill|lt=jōri system|Q11523645|qid=Q11523645|1=jōri system}}, it should be Toyoshima District rather than Kawabe District, meaning the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} made an error regarding the district.”Shiga Tsuyoshi, “Study of Shikinaisha, Vol. 3” (1977), {{National Bibliography Number|77010794}}. The “Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer” states: “The Ikota Shrine in Ikeda Town, Toyono District, Osaka Prefecture, stands at the site of the Kureori Shrine of Kureori Village. With the prosperity of the region in the medieval period, this shrine, which enshrined Anaori Ōkami, was relocated to the entrance of the town and revered as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}}. The Kureori Shrine was designated as its {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|qid=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}, and eventually the Kureori Shrine came to be called Ikota Shrine as well.”Yoshii Sadatoshi, “Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 5” (1977), {{National Bibliography Number|78005760}}. In terms of old place names, the Ikota Shrine enshrined at this location is considered to correspond to the original. In January {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 7 (1995), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|qid=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}}, and new buildings were reconstructed in April Heisei 11 (1999). File:Ikota-jinja, shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Ikota-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach path File:Ikota-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii gate === Ikota Kofun === {{Kofun in Japan |Name = Ikota Kofun |Image = |Alternative name = |Affiliation = |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}}Shimosakabe 4-chōme |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|49.70|N|135|26|1.70|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Ikota Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|Q11268718|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |Size = Mound length 92 m |Burial facility = Unknown |Excavated items = |Satellite tombs = |Construction period = Late {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|qid=Q8095|1=5th century}} |Interred = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = None |Tangible cultural property = |Notes = The mound no longer exists |Map = |Icon = |Label = |Label position = }} '''Ikota Kofun''' (いこたこふん) was a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} that once stood within the precincts of Ikota Shrine. Its shape was a {{ill|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|Q11268718|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. Today, the burial mound has been lost. The tomb was keyhole-shaped, with the front portion facing south. The mound dimensions are as follows (based on a survey conducted in {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|qid=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 38)){{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: approx. 92 meters * Rear circular portion diameter: approx. 46 meters * Front portion width: approx. 53 meters This makes it the largest kofun in Amagasaki City. A surrounding moat encircled the mound, measuring approximately 20 meters in width{{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. No excavated artifacts are known.[http://www2.city.amagasaki.hyogo.jp/bunkazai/siseki/ikota_kofun/ikota_kofun.html Ikota Kofun] (Amagasaki City website). This Ikota Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|qid=Q8095|1=5th century}}, during the middle {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|qid=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The interred individual is unknown, but Ikota Shrine maintains that it is the tomb of the ancestral deity of the Sakaibe no Muraji (Sakaibe clan), and that the shrine was built atop this tomb.Shrine origin document. On the south side of the front portion, there were once two mounds known as Samurai-zuka and Hakuba-zuka, which are said to have been {{ill|lt=satellite tombs|Q11658220|qid=Q11658220|1=Baizuka}} of Ikota Kofun. However, to date, archaeological excavations have not produced definitive evidence proving that Ikota Kofun was indeed a kofun.“The Inagawa River Basin in the Kofun Period – Movements of the Kinai Polity Projected onto the Inagawa Basin – (Ikeda City Museum of History and Folklore, Heisei 22 Special Exhibition),” Ikeda City Museum of History and Folklore, 2010. === Excavation history === * {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 25): Test excavation at the center of the rear circular portion. No clear structures or artifacts confirmed{{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. * {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|qid=Q2755|1=1963}} (Shōwa 38): Survey measurement{{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. * From {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|qid=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40) onward: Land readjustment projects significantly altered the moat area{{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. * Recent years: Excavations in the presumed moat area. No structures or artifacts confirmed{{Sfn|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. === Access === * 15-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tsukaguchi Station (JR West)|Q847972|qid=Q847972|lt=Tsukaguchi Station|1=Tsukaguchi Station (JR West)}} on the {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|qid=Q1154538|lt=JR Takarazuka Line|1=Fukuchiyama Line}}. * Immediately after getting off at Chikamatsu Park on the city bus. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine origin document * On-site explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Masuda Hiyoshi|year=2002|chapter=Ikota Kofun|title={{ill|lt=Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|Q21654087|qid=Q21654087|lt=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun}}|publisher={{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|qid=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Ikota Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ikota-jinja}} * [http://www2.city.amagasaki.hyogo.jp/bunkazai/siseki/ikota_kofun/ikota_kofun.html Ikota Kofun] – Amagasaki City website {{Redirect category affiliation |redirect1=Ikota Kofun |1-1=Kofun in Hyōgo Prefecture |1-2=History of Amagasaki City |1-3=Zenpō-kōen-fun }} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikota Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikota Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Amagasaki]] [[Category:尼崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]]
